case.title: TACOMA ASSOCIATION OF CREDIT MEN v. WASHINGTON

opinion.h1: TACOMA ASSOCIATION OF CREDIT MEN v. WASHINGTON, 377 U.S. 532 (1964)
opinion.h4: TACOMA ASSOCIATION OF CREDIT MEN v. WASHINGTON. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF WASHINGTON. No. 995. Decided June 8, 1964.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
John J. O'Connell, Attorney General of Washington, and James A. Furber and Henry W. Wager, Assistant Attorneys General, for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
